context.summary: Turn your micro:bit into a âhot potatoâ by coding it to become a random timer. To play the game, pass the âmicro:bit potatoâ on to the next person before the timer goes off.
improve_it: Change the duration of the timer to suit different tasks or change from random to a specific length of time.
Modify the program so a different icon or your own picture appears when you press button A.
Change the sound to a happy/positive sound to indicate when someone has âwonâ something.
Use it in different contexts, for examples, to practise spellings or times tables, or use as a class countdown.

code.source:
from microbit import *
import music, random

while True:
    if button_a.is_pressed():
        timer = random.randint(5, 15)
        display.show(Image.CHESSBOARD)
        while timer > 0:
            timer -= 1
            sleep(1000)
        display.show(Image.NO)
        music.play(music.WAWAWAWAA, wait=False)
        audio.play(Sound.SAD)
